                                    THE REGULATION OF FINANCIAL PRIVACY

                                       0 if there are exemptions and 1 otherwise.

4. Enforcement possibilities and remedies
4.1 Penalties of non-registration   This category emphasises the fact that registration may only be enforced via
                                    the introduction and use of legal powers to achieve a compliance with the
                                    law on the side of data controllers.
4.2 Existence of penalties for      This category emphasises that there must be an explicit penalty for receiving
unauthorised receiving of personal  personal information in an unauthorised way (like misrepresenting the own
information                         identity).
4.3 Existence of penalties for      This category emphasises that there must be an explicit penalty for
unauthorised storage                unauthorised storage of personal information.
4.4 Existence of penalties for      This category states that there must be an explicit penalty for an unlawful
unauthorised communication of       disclosure of any personal information.
personal information
4.5 Existence of penalties for         This category emphasises that there must be an explicit penalty for the
communication of incorrect             knowing communication of false information on the side of credit bureaus or
information                            information furnishers.
4.6 Existence of penalties for         This category indicates that there are explicit penalties for the unauthorised
unauthorised data modification         modification of personal information.
4.7 Existence of penalty in the form   This category further differentiates in stating the kind of penalty received by
of a (monetary) fine                   a responsible subject. If there is a penalty in the form of a fine, we denote a
                                       1.
4.8 Existence of penalty in the form   Strict regimes should imply that there is also a high penalty in the form of
of imprisonment                        imprisonment in case of the infringement of the rights of a data subject.

59
                                                     NICOLA JENTZSCH
